name: Claude Code Subagent Spawner
description: Spawn parallel or sequential Claude Code subagents via the Agent tool to execute discrete work units. Enables orchestrators to distribute tasks across multiple agents without external infrastructure.

inputs.description: Discrete work units from TASKS.md or fleet config to distribute across subagents.
Layer-specific AGENT.md content and quality policy references to inject into each subagent context.

outputs.description: Completed work artifacts from subagents: PRs, evaluation reports, signals.
FLEET-REPORT.md summarizing all subagent runs with status, outputs, and blockers.

permissions.justification: Orchestrator reads missions and tasks; spawned subagents write their respective outputs (branches, PRs, evaluations).

constraints: Maximum 5 concurrent subagents per orchestration run — aligns with mission-orchestration skill constraint.
Maximum subagent depth is 1 — subagents must not spawn further subagents.
Independent tasks must run in parallel; dependent tasks must run sequentially (wait for results before proceeding).
Each subagent must receive: task context, layer AGENT.md reference, and relevant quality policy references.
Orchestrator must write a FLEET-REPORT.md to the mission folder summarizing all subagent outcomes.
Failed subagent runs must produce a signal in work/signals/ — never silently discard failures.
Orchestrator must not execute tasks directly — it decomposes and delegates only.

changelog.change: Initial version. Claude Code native subagent spawning skill using the Agent tool for parallel mission execution.
